title: Spicy Crunchy Spanish Fish Croquetas
description: These crispy, spicy, and savory Spanish-inspired fish croquetas are perfect for a quick snack or light meal. They feature a halal-friendly yuzu-cure and a crunchy coating that adds texture and flavor.
instructions: Cut the white fish into small pieces and season with salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper.
In a bowl, whisk together the flour and a pinch of salt. Add the fish pieces and toss to coat, breaking up any clumps with your fingers.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the yuzu-cure and a pinch of salt. Add the egg and whisk until smooth.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the coated fish pieces and cook until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Transfer the cooked fish to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a separate skillet, toast the panko breadcrumbs over medium heat until lightly browned and fragrant.
In a bowl, mix together the toasted breadcrumbs, dark brown sugar, smoked paprika, and chopped parsley.
Using your hands, shape the breadcrumb mixture into small patties, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Dip each patty into the cooled fish mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
Shape the mixture into small oval shapes, about 1 1/2 inches long.
Dip each oval shape into the yuzu-cure mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
Roll the oval shape in the toasted breadcrumb mixture to coat.
Heat about 1-2 inches of olive oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry the coated fish patties until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Drain the croquetas on paper towels and serve hot, garnished with lemon juice and additional parsley if desired.
If you want a lighter version, you can bake the croquetas in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for about 10-12 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
